findings.title: Day-Journaling modal is 100% hardcoded English (title, prompt, placeholder, buttons, toast) — no i18n at all
findings.rootCause: journalingModal.ts never imports `t` and emits raw English literals in its innerHTML and showLiquidAlert call. It is the only home-modal that was never migrated to i18n (dayDetailModal.ts has ~40 t() calls, tripMediaModals.ts ~64, tripChecklistModal.ts is fully localized). No locale keys exist for any of these strings (grep for 'Save Story'/'Memories saved'/'Capture your memories' in en.ts returns nothing). It is reachably wired from TripBody.tsx:305-307 via the `.day-journaling-btn` delegate.

findings.title: Day-pin map toasts ('Click on the map…', 'Location saved!', 'Day deleted') are hardcoded English
findings.rootCause: handlers.ts imports `t` (line 28) and uses it elsewhere, but these three showLiquidAlert() calls pass bare English string literals. No locale keys exist for them (grep for 'Location saved'/'Click on the map'/'Day deleted' in en.ts returns nothing).

findings.title: PDF export modal: 'Expenses', 'Settle up', 'Photos' option labels + descriptions hardcoded in English (acknowledged shortcut)
findings.rootCause: Deliberate shortcut documented in-code at modals/pdf.ts:74-77 ('The modal labels are English literals because the brief forbids touching the locale files; the PDF CONTENT is localised server-side'). The renderPdfOption() calls at lines 78-83 pass raw English strings instead of t(). The localized PDF body does not cover this client-side modal UI.

findings.title: Trip-picker shows hardcoded English 'No Active Trips' in navbar/sidebar when user has no trips
findings.rootCause: updateTripSelector() runs on every state:changed and overwrites the select's innerHTML with `'<option value="">No Active Trips</option>'` (trip-controls.ts:45) — a hardcoded literal, so even a data-i18n attribute on the static option would be clobbered. The module imports `t` (used at line 93+) but not for this string; no key exists for it. The index.html `<option>Select a Trip...</option>` (lines 513/543) also has no data-i18n-key.

findings.title: Localized population-fact templates produce ungrammatical 'thousand' phrasing in ES/PT/FR ('77 mil de personas', '77 mille d'habitants')
findings.rootCause: localizeFact() (place-names.ts:30-41) re-formats the parsed fact through a SINGLE template (facts.country/facts.state) that hardcodes the partitive particle ('de {unit} de personas' / '{unit} d'habitants') which is grammatically correct only for the 'million' unit. The 'thousand' unit (facts.unitThousand = 'mil'/'mil'/'mille') does not take the particle in these languages, so the fixed template is wrong for every thousand-scale country.

findings.title: Home-hero inspirational travel quotes are hardcoded English for the no-trip slideshow (all locales)
findings.rootCause: In the no-trip branch, slideshow.ts:94 sets `quotes = INSPIRATIONAL_PAIRS.map(p => p.q)` and renders them verbatim — there is no localization path for `.q` (unlike the active-trip path, which runs facts through localizeFact). The quotes live as English literals in constants.ts:69-82 / TRAVEL_DATA_DEFAULT (constants.ts:85). The active-trip path intentionally ignores `.q` (slideshow.ts:162-168), so only the no-trip path leaks.

findings.title: Systematic hardcoded English in React title/aria-label/alt attributes across ~15 components
findings.rootCause: These components localize their visible text/options via t() but left the title=, aria-label=, and one alt= attribute as English string literals. The repo is otherwise disciplined (no hardcoded placeholder=); these attributes were missed. For sighted users the title= tooltips are visibly English; for screen-reader users the aria-labels are announced in English even after <html lang> is switched, so VoiceOver/NVDA pronounce them with the wrong-language phonemes.

findings.title: Hardcoded English error toasts in Feed and Archived-trip share (bypass t())
findings.rootCause: Both call showLiquidAlert() with bare English literals / template strings instead of routing through t(). The app already has parallel localized toast keys (e.g. errors.commentPostFailed, errors.deleteFailed) but these two paths were not migrated.

findings.title: Batch-import provider option labels ('Tricount Export (CSV/XLSX)', 'Splitwise Export') hardcoded English in the dropdown
findings.rootCause: POPULARS is a module-level array (BatchUpload.tsx:27-30) with hardcoded English `name` values; the rest of the component uses t(). Brand names ('Tricount'/'Splitwise') should stay, but the descriptive 'Export (CSV/XLSX)' / 'Export' suffixes are untranslated.

findings.title: a11y skip-link 'Skip to content' is hardcoded English (no data-i18n binding)
findings.rootCause: The skip-link in index.html has no data-i18n-key attribute, so paintI18nBindings (which hydrates [data-i18n-key] nodes) never localizes it. Every other nav label in index.html carries a data-i18n-key; this one was missed.

findings.title: Capital/biggest-city names baked into fact data use non-localized exonyms ('Wien', 'Alger') shown in all locales
findings.rootCause: localizeFact (place-names.ts:36-41) only localizes the surrounding template; the {capital}/{biggest} slot values come verbatim from the English source data in constants.ts, which itself contains inconsistent exonyms ('Wien', 'Alger'). There is no per-language city-name table (intentional, to avoid ~960 strings), so the data's own inconsistency leaks to every locale.
